{{Quote|Transmissions we've intercepted indicated that many of the captured scientists will be transferred to Kessel. We will not leave them to die in the darkness of the spice mines.|[[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] briefing [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]] on the Kessel operation|<ref name="EaW" />}}
The '''Space Battle in the Kessel Asteroid Field''' was a rescue operation during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Prelude==
{{Quote|Those craft may help us but I fear for the citizens of Fresia and Incom's scientists. The Empire may make an example of them to keep other systems in line...|Mon Mothma|<ref name="EaW" />}}
After the [[Battle of Fresia]], in which the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] extracted four [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] starfighter prototypes from an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] depot on [[Fresia/Legends|Fresia]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] retaliated by taking a number of [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom]] scientists hostage. The Imperial convoy transporting the hostages passed through [[Maw Cluster/Legends|The Maw]] and prepared to land on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] to deliver the scientists to the infamous prisons.

The Rebel Alliance quickly learned of this. Led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]], the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] sent a task force to [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] consisting of a number of [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] and the [[Sundered Heart/Legends|''Sundered Heart'']]. The [[Bomber/Legends|bombers]] would prove invaluable during the operation to disable the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|Imperial Shuttles]] carrying the scientists, thus preventing them from descending into the planet's atmosphere.

==Battle==
{{Quote|If we can get to those shuttles during the transfer, we may be able to pull the prisoners right out from under the Empire's nose!|Captain Antilles|<ref name="EaW" />}}
[[File:Space_Battle_in_the_Kessel_Asteroid_Field.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] engaging an [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]].]]

The Rebels ambushed the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] in a narrow corridor through an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] field. While the [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]] and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] engaged the Imperial ships in combat, the ''Sundered Heart'' and a team of Y-wings disabled the shuttles.

The Y-wings opened fire with their [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], disabling the first transports. Moving quickly, the ''Sundered Heart'' docked with the shuttles. A small commando team boarded the shuttles and rescued the scientists. However, the Empire was determined to not allow the scientists to fall into Rebel hands, going so far as attempting to destroy the shuttles before they could be boarded. A call for reinforcements was issued by the Imperial commander, and two [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] were detected entering the system.

As the last shuttle was boarded, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] reinforcements finally arrived in the system and began to overwhelm the small Rebel force. With not a moment to lose, the Rebels cleared the asteroid field and jumped to lightspeed.

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|The rescued Incom scientists have provided us with a wealth of scientific knowledge that will benefit all of the Rebellion. We are fortunate to have them on our side.|Mon Mothma|<ref name="EaW" />}}
The freed [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom]] scientists would later prove to be invaluable to the Alliance. Their schematics allowed the Rebellion to launch the production of X-wings.<ref name="TNEGtVaV">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
This battle was the fourth mission of the Rebel campaign in the [[2006]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''.
